name: AZQ Stage 2 Wave D Checks
purpose: Define behavior-first checks for the Stage 2 Wave D refactor-prep wave so Formam can be cleaned up for Stage 3 without drifting from the live Stage 2 baseline.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the refactor.

baseline_checks.name: stage_regression_suite
baseline_checks.description: Verify the existing regression suite still passes.

baseline_checks.name: formam_imports
baseline_checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import azq.formam.storage
import azq.formam.build
import azq.formam.deliverables
import azq.formam.maps
PY
baseline_checks.description: Verify the current public Formam modules still import cleanly.

baseline_checks.name: ritual_script_parses
baseline_checks.description: Verify the ritual runner shell script still parses.

STAGE2-WAVED-01.description: Split Formam path and schema primitives out of storage.py while preserving the Stage 2 public surface.

checks.name: paths_and_schemas_modules_exist
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
assert Path('azq/formam/paths.py').is_file(), 'missing azq/formam/paths.py'
assert Path('azq/formam/schemas.py').is_file(), 'missing azq/formam/schemas.py'
PY
checks.description: Verify the new paths and schemas modules exist.

checks.name: paths_and_schemas_expose_expected_helpers
checks.description: Verify the extracted modules expose the core path and schema helpers while storage.py remains import-compatible.

human_checks: Keep path constants and normalization primitives in the extracted modules, not smeared across storage.py.
storage.py should still feel like a compatibility and repository surface, not a junk drawer.

STAGE2-WAVED-02.description: Extract deliverable storage helpers into a focused deliverable storage module without breaking the Stage 2 build/list/show path.

checks.name: deliverable_storage_module_exists
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
assert Path('azq/formam/deliverable_storage.py').is_file(), 'missing azq/formam/deliverable_storage.py'
PY
checks.description: Verify the deliverable storage module exists.

checks.name: deliverable_storage_round_trip_works
checks.description: Verify the extracted deliverable storage layer can normalize, write, read, and list canonical deliverables.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.formam import deliverable_storage
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    root = Path(tmp)
    deliverable_storage.FORM_DIR = root / 'data' / 'form'
    deliverable_storage.DELIVERABLES_DIR = deliverable_storage.FORM_DIR / 'deliverables'
    deliverable_storage.MAPS_DIR = deliverable_storage.FORM_DIR / 'maps'
    deliverable_storage.ensure_form_dirs()
    record = {
        'deliverable_id': 'FORM_001',
        'goal_id': 'FINIS_001',
        'title': 'First deliverable',
        'artifact_description': 'Describe the artifact.',
        'dependencies': [],
        'status': 'defined'
    }
    path = deliverable_storage.write_deliverable(record)
    assert path.is_file(), 'deliverable file was not written'
    loaded = deliverable_storage.load_deliverable('FORM_001')
    assert loaded['deliverable_id'] == 'FORM_001'
    assert loaded['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'
    assert loaded['title'] == 'First deliverable'
    all_items = deliverable_storage.load_all_deliverables()
    assert len(all_items) == 1, all_items

checks.name: build_and_deliverables_modules_still_import
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import azq.formam.build
import azq.formam.deliverables
PY
checks.description: Verify the build/list/show path still imports cleanly after the extraction.

human_checks: The extracted deliverable module should own deliverable repository behavior, not just re-export one big storage blob.
The Stage 2 public commands should still read as thin command or presentation layers over storage.

STAGE2-WAVED-03.description: Extract goal-map storage helpers into a focused map storage module and move dependency-edge logic closer to map ownership.

checks.name: map_storage_module_exists
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
assert Path('azq/formam/map_storage.py').is_file(), 'missing azq/formam/map_storage.py'
PY
checks.description: Verify the goal-map storage module exists.

checks.name: map_storage_round_trip_and_edges_work
checks.description: Verify the extracted map storage layer can normalize, write, read, and preserve dependency edges in a canonical goal map.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.formam import map_storage
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    root = Path(tmp)
    map_storage.FORM_DIR = root / 'data' / 'form'
    map_storage.DELIVERABLES_DIR = map_storage.FORM_DIR / 'deliverables'
    map_storage.MAPS_DIR = map_storage.FORM_DIR / 'maps'
    map_storage.ensure_form_dirs()
    record = {
        'goal_id': 'FINIS_001',
        'deliverables': [
            {'deliverable_id': 'FORM_001', 'title': 'One'},
            {'deliverable_id': 'FORM_002', 'title': 'Two'}
        ],
        'dependency_edges': [
            {'from': 'FORM_001', 'to': 'FORM_002'}
        ],
        'status': 'formed'
    }
    path = map_storage.write_goal_map(record)
    assert path.is_file(), 'goal map file was not written'
    loaded = map_storage.load_goal_map('FINIS_001')
    assert loaded['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'
    assert loaded['dependency_edges'] == [{'from': 'FORM_001', 'to': 'FORM_002'}], loaded
    all_maps = map_storage.load_all_goal_maps()
    assert len(all_maps) == 1, all_maps

checks.name: maps_module_still_imports
checks.description: Verify the map refresh and inspection path still imports cleanly after the extraction.

human_checks: Dependency-edge derivation should feel like map behavior, not a stray helper trapped in a generic storage file.
The map storage surface should stay human-readable and visibly aligned with the Formam doctrine.

STAGE2-WAVED-04.description: Split azq/cli.py into thinner engine-aware routers without breaking the public command surface.

checks.name: engine_router_modules_exist
checks.description: Verify each engine package now has at least one router-like module for CLI dispatch.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
candidates = ['router.py', 'commands.py', 'cli_router.py']
for package in ['azq/scintilla', 'azq/finis', 'azq/formam']:
    pkg = Path(package)
    assert any((pkg / candidate).is_file() for candidate in candidates), f'missing router-like module in {package}'

checks.name: top_level_cli_still_runs_and_dispatches
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import azq.cli
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/cli.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
assert 'def main' in text
assert 'from azq.' in text or 'import azq.' in text
PY
checks.description: Verify the top-level CLI still imports and remains the user-facing entrypoint after router extraction.

human_checks: This task is about thinner boundaries, not cosmetic shuffling. The top-level CLI should be easier to read after the refactor.
Do not over-fragment dispatch logic into tiny files that make navigation worse.

STAGE2-WAVED-05.description: Rename the stage-aware runner to a stage-agnostic name while preserving a compatibility path and keeping the ritual script honest.

checks.name: new_runner_exists
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
assert Path('azq_codex_task_runner.py').is_file(), 'missing azq_codex_task_runner.py'
PY
checks.description: Verify the new stage-agnostic runner exists.

checks.name: old_and_new_runner_can_both_report
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import subprocess
import sys
commands = [
    [sys.executable, 'azq_codex_task_runner.py', 'report', '--workspace', '.'],
    [sys.executable, 'azq_codex_stage1_task_runner.py', 'report', '--workspace', '.']
]
for cmd in commands:
    proc = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    assert proc.returncode == 0, (cmd, proc.stdout, proc.stderr)
PY
checks.description: Verify both the new runner and the compatibility entrypoint can still produce a report.

checks.name: ritual_script_mentions_new_runner
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('run_next_task_in_current_wave.sh').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
assert 'azq_codex_task_runner.py' in text, 'ritual runner does not reference new task runner name'
PY
checks.description: Verify the ritual script points at the new primary runner name.

human_checks: The old runner name should degrade into compatibility, not remain the conceptual center of the system.
Runbook and ritual wording should make the new runner name feel like the primary tool.

STAGE2-WAVED-06.description: Extend regression coverage so the Wave D refactor proves no Stage 2 Formam behavior drift.

checks.name: stage2_refactor_regression_tests_exist
checks.description: Verify there is explicit test coverage for the major Wave D refactor surfaces.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
candidate_files = [Path('tests/test_stage2_wave_d.py'), Path('tests/test_stage2_wave_c.py')]
existing = [path for path in candidate_files if path.is_file()]
assert existing, 'missing Stage 2 refactor regression test file'
text = '\n'.join(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8') for path in existing)
for token in ['paths', 'schemas', 'deliverable_storage', 'map_storage']:
    assert token in text, f'missing Wave D surface in tests: {token}'

checks.name: stage2_regression_suite_still_passes_after_refactor
checks.description: Verify the full regression suite still passes after the Wave D refactor.

human_checks: The new tests should exercise preserved behavior, not merely prove that files were renamed.
Wave D should leave Stage 2 safer for Stage 3, not just rearranged.
